steps: Recipe for 4 mL: %s %s Component Molarity / Concentration Amount Final concentration Tris-HCl, pH 7.5 1 M 2 mL 500 mM MgCl2 1 M 200 µL 50 mM dATP 100 mM 40 µL 1 mM dCTP 100 mM 40 µL 1 mM dGTP 100 mM 40 µL 1 mM dTTP 100 mM 40 µL 1 mM DTT 1 M 200 µL 50 mM PEG-8000 - 1 g 25 % NAD+ 100 mM 200 µL 5 mM H2O - to final volume of 4 mL
Mix dNTPs, NAD + , Tris-HCl, MgCl 2 and DTT.
Slowly add PEG-8000 to mixture and mix well, until completely dissolved. Add H 2 O to a final volume of 4 mL.
Prepare aliquots of the 5x isothermal buffer as required, e.g. 100 µL. Store at -20 ˚C.

Recipe for 25 x 15 µL aliquots: %s %s Component Concentration Amount Final concentration (after adding DNA) 5x isothermal rxn buffer 5x 100 µL 1x Taq DNA Ligase 40 U/µL 50 µL 4 U/µL T5 Exonuclease 1 U/µL 2 µL 4 U/mL Q5 Hi-Fi DNA Polymerase 2 U/µL 6.25 µL 25 U/mL H2O 216.75 A B C D 1   Total volume   300 µL   Total volume 375 µL
Work on ice. Mix H 2 O and 5x buffer, then add enzymes.
Prepare 20 x 15 µL aliquots in PCR tubes. Store at -20 ˚C. These aliquots are concentrated 1.33 x - add your DNA in a volume of 5 µL to a final volume/concentration of 20 µL / 1x.
After addition of DNA, incubate Gibson assembly mix at 50 ˚C for 2 hours. 00:00:00, Gibson assembly
Transform chemically competent cells with an aliquot of your assembly mix.
creator: Tatsuya Sakaguchi
